Cohle (still undercover as "Crash") and Ginger meet with DeWall Ledoux, Reggie's cook partner, in a roadside bar to discuss a drug deal. DeWall senses something dark in Cohle, telling him "I can see your soul... it's corrosive, like acid," and refuses the deal. After DeWall leaves, Hart follows him to a remote compound hidden in the bayou.

Cohle and Hart approach the property through brush, avoiding explosive booby traps. They storm the compound: Hart apprehends Reggie Ledoux and handcuffs him, while Cohle draws on DeWall. While searching the premises, Hart discovers two kidnapped children — a boy who died less than 24 hours ago and a catatonic girl — being held in horrific conditions. Enraged, Hart executes Ledoux with a point-blank shot to the head. Ledoux's last words reference "black stars" and "Carcosa." DeWall panics, flees, and is killed when he triggers one of his own land mines. Cohle fires an assault rifle into the forest to create evidence supporting a false narrative of a shootout. Both detectives are commended as heroes for rescuing the surviving child.

Seven years later in 2002, Hart has reconciled with Maggie and been promoted, while Cohle has a girlfriend named Laurie and is a renowned interrogator. However, Hart's teenage daughter Audrey is acting out — wearing dark clothing, smoking, engaging in sexual activity with older boys. Marty slaps her when she curses at him.

Cohle interrogates a prisoner named Guy Francis, who confesses to robbery and murder but then demands a deal, claiming that Ledoux was not the real killer. Francis says the actual killer is connected to powerful people and that he has heard of "the Yellow King" — information withheld from the public in the Dora Lange case. Shortly after, Francis is found dead of apparent suicide in his cell, following a phone call purportedly from his lawyer that actually came from a remote payphone near a water treatment plant. Cohle identifies one of the officers who escorted Francis as being named Childress — connected to the original Marie Fontenot disappearance.

Cohle visits the abandoned Light of the Way school and discovers lattice stick sculptures, white-bodied figures painted on walls with red markings, and a mural of black stars — all symbols of the Yellow King mythology.

In 2012, Papania and Gilbough tell Hart that they suspect Cohle has been behind the killings all along — that he conveniently led Hart to every clue. Cohle was photographed among bystanders at the recent Lake Charles crime scene, and is also a person of interest in the suspicious death of Reverend Tuttle two years earlier. They demand access to Cohle's storage unit, which he refuses without a warrant.
